subject: Ecology
title: Fens
description: A fast breakdown of Fens for builders.

hook.text: A fen looks like a bog but has a secret advantage—groundwater brings in mineral nutrients.

buildup.text: Groundwater-fed fens are less acidic than bogs and support richer plant communities.

discovery.text: Fens host rare orchids, sedges, and mosses that depend on mineral-rich but low-nutrient water.

twist.text: Draining a fen destroys it permanently—once the peat dries and oxidizes, it can't regenerate.

climax.text: Fens in Europe have declined by over 90% since 1900 due to drainage for agriculture.

punchline.text: A fen is a bog with better plumbing—and rarer species.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between a fen and a bog?
options: Fens have no water at all
